semanticCore: Protecting, preserving, guarding, or fearing God
quranicRelevance: The root of (Taqwa), which is the most central moral-spiritual concept in the Quran. It covers: protecting oneself from harm/hellfire, guarding against sin, mindfulness/fear of God (Taqwa), and the pious/righteous (Muttaqin) who shield themselves from divine displeasure.

keyDerivations.meaning: To protect, guard, or save from harm
Piety, mindfulness of God, or self-protection from divine displeasure

figurativeOrRareUsages: اتَّقَى (Form VIII Verb): To fear, be on one's guard, or shield oneself.
discardedNoise: A horse walking carefully because of hoof pain.
summaryEn: The root encompasses protection and guarding, giving rise to (Taqwa)—the Quranic cornerstone of spiritual mindfulness, righteousness, and shield against divine wrath.
summaryAr: يشمل هذا الجذر معاني الحماية والوقاية، وهو مصدر التقوى؛ التي تمثل الركيزة القرآنية للوعي الروحي والبر والوقاية من غضب الله.
rawText: أُوقِيَّةٌ An ounce: see رِطْلٌ, in two places. اِتَّقى 8 He preserved, or guarded, himself exceedingly, or extraordinarily: (Ksh, Bd in ii. 1:) he put a thing between him and another to preserve him, or guard him. (Ham, p. 359.) === In the conventional language of the law, He preserved, or guarded, himself exceedingly, or extraordinarily, (Ksh, Bd, ubi supra,) from sin, of commission or of omission, (Ksh,) [or] from what would harm him in the world to come. (Bd, who describes three degrees.) It may often be rendered He was pious; or careful of his religious duties. === اِتَّقَى عَلَيْهِمْ وَحَذِرَ [He guarded himself against them in an extraordinary degree, and was cautious, or wary]. (JK, TA, in art. خشى.) See 1. === يَتْقِى, for يَتَّقِى: see art. تقى: and see a verse of Khufáf Ibn-Nudbeh, (quoted in the S, in art. وقى,) cited voce أَثْرٌ. === يَتَّقْ for يَتَّقِ: see a verse cited voce مُتَأَوِّبٌ. تَوَقَّاهُ 5 (and, accord. to a usage in the K, art. حرز, also تَوَقَّى مِنْهُ) He guarded against it; was cautious of it; syn. اِحْتَرَزَ منه and تَحَرَّزَ منه. (S, K, * art. حرز.) === تَبَقَّهْ وَتَوَقَّهْ: see 4 in art. بقى. وَاقٍ A saddle that does not gall the back. (S, K.) وَاقِيَةٌ used as an inf. n., like بَاقِيَّةٌ: see Har, p. 136. === وَاقِيَةٌ as meaning one preserver: see an ex. voce بَاقِيَةٌ. وَقَاهُ ٱللّٰهُ السُّوْءَ 1 God preserved him from evil. (Msb.) See a verse cited voce نَقَشَ. === وَقَاكَ اللّٰهُ كُلَّ مَحْذُور God preserve thee from all fear: see مَحْذُورٌ. === قِ, or قِهْ, and تَوَقَّ, are imperatives [meaning Be thou cautious]. (JK.) === قِ عَلَى ظَلْعِكَ: see ظَلَعَ. === وَقَاهُ and 🡻اِتَّقَاهُ both signify He was cautious of it; syn. حَذِرَهُ. (K.) وَقِىٌّ Cautious, guarding himself from sin, &c.: see تَقِىٌّ. وَقِيَّةٌ Property by means of which one preserves himself: pl. وَقِيَّاتٌ. (TA.) See a verse cited voce مَحْبَلٌ. وِقَايَةٌ Excess of preserving or guarding. (Ksh, Bd in ii. 1.) === A preservative.
